body: review-spec 2026-06-21 APPROVE — substrate reconciled (choice 0007 refined to verify-auto-accept, 0009 resolved analyst-loads-relate-reads, card 0002 scenarios updated). Carry into implement: (1) LOW pin the 'high-confidence' auto-accept threshold — define the mid-confidence→suggested boundary (fixture case d). (2) LOW move duckdb dev→runtime dep; PR reviewer confirms choice-0001 read-only boundary holds (per choice 0014). (3) LOW ac-05 extends TableSchema (currently fields-only, Serialize-only) to carry foreignKeys + custom props and still validate against the vendored profile; DDL read-back path.

ac-06 DEVIATION (2026-06-21): DuckDB does NOT support ALTER TABLE ADD FOREIGN KEY ('Not implemented'); FK constraints only work at CREATE-table time. So 'DDL applies cleanly via ALTER' is impossible. Resolution: emit standard portable ALTER...ADD CONSTRAINT FK DDL (the legible artifact; Postgres/standard SQL accept it), and prove-it-holds in the ac-06 test via a CREATE-time-FK round-trip (rebuild parent w/ PK + child w/ FK, re-insert the verified data — DuckDB enforces and it succeeds because the edge was verified). Stronger than re-running our own orphan check.

review-pr cycle 1 REQUEST_CHANGES addressed: (blocker) ac-07 CLI now writes the descriptor — relate::build_descriptor + run_path, dovetail relate <db> --out <path> writes a Frictionless descriptor with foreignKeys inside Table Schema; (ask 1) new test validates the FK-carrying descriptor against the vendored profile via the jsonschema validator; (ask 2) card 0002 notes self-referential + composite keys out of scope. Bin stays thin (duckdb stays in core via run_path).
drive completed: relate (discover→verify→render) shipped, 7/7 ACs, verify-auto-accept design, both reviews APPROVE (review-pr 2 cycles), PR #3
